dashboard.step1: Configure your media server connection in Settings → System
dashboard.step2: Select which libraries to sync in Settings → Libraries
dashboard.step3: Run the "Sync Movies" job in Jobs to import your movie library
dashboard.step4: Enable users who should receive AI recommendations in Users
dashboard.step5: Run the "Generate Embeddings" job to create movie embeddings
dashboard.step6: Run the "Generate Recommendations" job to create personalized picks

gaps.pageSubtitle: Compare TMDB movie collections against your synced library to find missing titles. Requests via Seerr are never automatic — you choose what to request.
gaps.statusTmdbBad: Add your TMDb API key in Admin → Settings
gaps.statusCollectionsBad: Run Enrich Metadata (Admin → Jobs) so movies get collection_id from TMDb
gaps.statusSeerrBad: Configure Seerr in Admin → Settings to request missing titles
gaps.inProgressScan: In progress — {{scanned}} collections scanned, {{missing}} missing released titles so far, ~{{pct}}% released parts in library.
gaps.lastRun: Last run: {{when}} — {{scanned}} collections, {{missing}} missing released titles, ~{{pct}}% released parts in library. Re-run after syncing new movies.
gaps.noRunYet: No run yet. Start an analysis to scan your library against TMDb collections.
gaps.scanningCollections: Scanning collections — results appear below as each batch finishes.
gaps.missingSoFar: {{count}} missing released title so far — the grid updates as analysis runs.
gaps.missingSoFar_plural: {{count}} missing released titles so far — the grid updates as analysis runs.
gaps.noMissingInSnapshot: No missing titles in this snapshot (or nothing matches the filter).
gaps.dialogBulkBody: This will send {{count}} request(s) to Seerr using your linked account (Radarr options already chosen). You can track them under My Requests.

usersPage.errorLoadUsers: Failed to load users from media server
usersPage.aiOverrideOn: {{name}} can now control their AI explanation preference
usersPage.aiOverrideOff: {{name}} will use the global AI explanation setting
usersPage.discoverOff: {{name}} will no longer see discovery suggestions
usersPage.discoverReqOff: {{name}} can no longer request discovered content
usersPage.subtitleDesktop: Manage {{provider}} users and enable AI recommendations
usersPage.syncTooltip: Import new users and sync profile data (email, admin status) from media server
usersPage.syncTooltipShort: Sync new users from media server
usersPage.discoverColTooltip: Show discovery suggestions for content not in library
usersPage.requestColTooltip: Allow user to request discovered content via Seerr
usersPage.aiOverrideColTooltip: Allow user to control their AI explanation preference

userDetail.recsEmpty: No recommendations generated yet. Run the recommendations job to generate picks for this user.
userDetail.diagnosticsPlaceholder: Recommendation diagnostics and score breakdowns will appear here.
userDetail.seerrIdInvalid: Seerr user id must be a positive integer or empty to clear
userDetail.watchHistoryBody: Allow this user to mark movies and series as unwatched. This will update both the media server (Emby/Jellyfin) and Aperture's database.
userDetail.watchHistoryToggleHint: User can mark items as unwatched from their Watch History page and movie detail pages
userDetail.discoveryBody: Control this user's access to the Discovery feature, which suggests content not currently in your library based on their taste profile.
userDetail.discoverySuggestionsHint: User can view AI-powered suggestions for movies and series not in the library
userDetail.discoveryRequestHint: User can request discovered content to be added to the library via Seerr
userDetail.discoveryRequiresDiscover: Request permission requires Discovery to be enabled first.
userDetail.seerrMappingBody: Optional override: numeric user id from Seerr (Users in Seerr). When set, content requests are attributed to this Seerr account. Leave empty to rely on automatic matching by email or username.
userDetail.aiExplanationBody: Control whether this user can customize their AI explanation preferences.
userDetail.globalSettingEnabledAllowed: Global Setting: AI explanations are enabled by default (per-user overrides allowed)
userDetail.globalSettingEnabledDisallowed: Global Setting: AI explanations are enabled by default (per-user overrides disabled globally)
userDetail.globalSettingDisabledAllowed: Global Setting: AI explanations are disabled by default (per-user overrides allowed)
userDetail.globalSettingDisabledDisallowed: Global Setting: AI explanations are disabled by default (per-user overrides disabled globally)
userDetail.allowOverrideHint: When enabled, this user can toggle AI explanations on/off for their own recommendations
userDetail.overridesDisabledGlobally: Per-user overrides are disabled globally. Enable "Allow Per-User Overrides" in Settings → AI Config → Algorithm to grant users override permission.
userDetail.userChoseEnable: (User has chosen to enable AI explanations)
userDetail.userChoseDisable: (User has chosen to disable AI explanations)
userDetail.watchHistoryTabEmpty: No watch history found. Run the watch history sync job to import data from the media server.

jobsPage.subtitle: Manage syncing, AI processing, and system maintenance tasks

ui.cancelBody: Are you sure you want to stop {{name}}? The job will be cancelled and any progress will be lost.
ui.historyEmpty: No run history found for this job.
ui.configManualOnlyBody: This job deletes all existing data before rebuilding. It cannot be scheduled and must be run manually. Use only after major algorithm changes, embedding model changes, or if recommendations appear corrupted.
ui.configPreviewManualOnly: Manual only - this job cannot be scheduled

home.heroSubtitle: Here's what we've picked for you based on your watch history
home.topPicksSubtitle: AI-powered recommendations based on your taste
home.topPicksEmpty: No recommendations yet. Your personalized picks will appear here once they're generated.

insights.subtitleSeries: How similar this series is to series you've enjoyed

insights.tooltipVariety: How much variety this adds to your recommendations

insights.basedOnHistorySeries: Based on your history with similar series:

aiFunctionCard.toolCallingWarningTitle: This model doesn't support reliable tool calling.
aiFunctionCard.toolCallingWarningBody: The assistant will work but cannot search your library or make recommendations.
aiFunctionCard.toolCallingWarningOllama: For Ollama, use firefunction-v2 or qwen3 instead.
aiFunctionCard.embeddingUnsupported: This model doesn't support embeddings. Choose a different model.
aiFunctionCard.ollamaInstallTitle: Install models on your Ollama server
aiFunctionCard.baseUrlHelperOllama: Make sure Ollama is running and accessible at this URL
aiFunctionCard.addCustomDialog_ollama: Enter the exact model name as it appears on your Ollama server. You can find model names at ollama.com/library.
aiFunctionCard.addCustomDialog_openrouter: Enter the exact model name as it appears on your OpenRouter account. You can find model names at openrouter.ai/models (e.g., anthropic/claude-3.5-sonnet, openai/gpt-4o).
aiFunctionCard.addCustomDialog_huggingface: Enter the exact model name as it appears on Hugging Face Hub. You can find model names at huggingface.co/models (e.g., meta-llama/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V3-0324).
aiFunctionCard.addCustomDialog_compatible: Enter the exact model name as it appears on your OpenAI-compatible server.
aiFunctionCard.embeddingVectorTitle: Vector Dimension Must Match Model Output
aiFunctionCard.embeddingVectorBody: The embedding dimension you select must exactly match the number of dimensions your model outputs. If these don't match, embedding generation will fail or produce incorrect results.
aiFunctionCard.embeddingCommonDimensionsList:
384 — granite-embedding-30m-english, all-MiniLM-L6-v2
768 — nomic-embed-text, snowflake-arctic-embed-m, e5-base-v2
1024 — mxbai-embed-large, snowflake-arctic-embed-l, voyage-multilingual-2
1536 — OpenAI text-embedding-3-small, text-embedding-ada-002
3072 — OpenAI text-embedding-3-large
4096 — nv-embed-v2, larger custom models
aiFunctionCard.embeddingCheckDocs: Check your model's documentation to find the correct dimension.
aiFunctionCard.validatingModel: Validating model… This may take a moment{{ollamaSuffix}}

topPicksOutputConfig.accordionTitle: What's the difference between Library, Collection, and Playlist?
topPicksOutputConfig.libraryBody: Appears as a separate library in your media server sidebar. Contains virtual copies (STRM files) of the media. Best for dedicated browsing of Top Picks.
topPicksOutputConfig.collectionBody: Groups your existing library items into a "box set" that appears when browsing. Uses your original media files directly. Best for organization within existing libraries.
topPicksOutputConfig.playlistBody: Creates an ordered playlist in the Playlists section. Uses your original media files directly. Best for sequential watching or shuffle play.

studioDetail.emptyLibrary: No content found for {{name}} in your library.

search.subtitle: Find movies and series using smart search with filters

userSettings.profileSyncedCaption: Profile information above is synced from your media server.
userSettings.emailHelperCustom: You have set a custom email (not synced from Emby)
userSettings.emailHelperSynced: Synced from Emby Connect. Edit to override.
userSettings.emailNotificationsSubtitle: Receive email notifications about your recommendations
userSettings.aiLibraryNamesSubtitle: Customize how your AI recommendations libraries appear in your media server.
userSettings.libraryHelperNamedMovies: Your movies library will be named: "{{name}}"
userSettings.libraryHelperNamedSeries: Your series library will be named: "{{name}}"
userSettings.libraryHelperEmpty: Leave empty to use the global default template
userSettings.aiLibraryFooter: Changes will apply the next time the library sync jobs run or when recommendations are regenerated.
userSettings.aiExplanationSubtitle: Choose whether to include AI-generated explanations in your recommendation descriptions.
userSettings.aiExplanationIncludeBody: When enabled, each recommendation includes "Why Aperture picked this for you"
userSettings.aiExplanationFooter: Changes will apply when your recommendations are next regenerated.
userSettings.watchedRecsSubtitle: Choose whether to include content you've already watched in your AI recommendations.
userSettings.includeWatchedDesc: Recommendations may include movies and series you've already watched
userSettings.newContentOnlyDesc: Recommendations will only show content you haven't watched yet
userSettings.watchedRecsFooter: By default, recommendations only include new content. Enable this if you want rewatching suggestions.
userSettings.dislikedSubtitle: Choose how content you've rated 1-3 hearts should be handled in recommendations.
userSettings.excludeDislikedDesc: Content you dislike will never appear in recommendations
userSettings.penalizeDislikedDesc: Content you dislike will appear less often but may still show up occasionally
userSettings.dislikedFooter: Rate content with 1-3 hearts to mark it as disliked. Changes will apply when your recommendations are next regenerated.
userSettings.similarityGraphPrefsSubtitle: Customize how the similarity graph explores connections between movies and series.
userSettings.hideWatchedOn: Graph only shows unwatched content for new discoveries
userSettings.hideWatchedOff: Graph includes all similar content, even what you've already seen
userSettings.fullFranchiseOn: Show entire franchises without limits (e.g., all 26 James Bond films)
userSettings.fullFranchiseOff: Limit items per franchise to encourage diverse discoveries
userSettings.similarityGraphFooter: These preferences apply when exploring similar content on detail pages and in fullscreen graph view.
userSettings.traktSubtitle: Connect your Trakt account to sync your movie and TV show ratings.
userSettings.traktFooter: Syncing imports your Trakt ratings (1-10) as heart ratings in Aperture. Higher-rated content will have more influence on your recommendations.
userSettings.libraryNamesSaved: Library names saved! They will be used for future library updates.

algorithmSettings.weightSimilarityDesc: How much to prioritize content similar to what you've enjoyed
algorithmSettings.weightNoveltyDesc: How much to prioritize exploring new genres and styles
algorithmSettings.weightRatingDesc: How much to favor highly-rated content
algorithmSettings.weightDiversityDesc: How much variety to include in recommendations

settingsDatabase.title: Database Management
settingsDatabase.dangerBody: These actions are irreversible and will delete data.
settingsDatabase.purgeDescription: Purge all content data to start fresh. This will delete all movies, series, episodes, embeddings, watch history, ratings, recommendations, taste profiles, and assistant conversations. Library configuration and user accounts are preserved.
settingsDatabase.irreversibleBody: You are about to permanently delete ALL content from your database. This includes:

settingsMediaServer.fillFieldsBeforeTest: Please fill in all fields before testing

settingsMediaServer.securityWarningBody: Only enable this if your Aperture instance is on a private network with no internet exposure. If your server is accessible via reverse proxy or the internet, leave this disabled to prevent unauthorized access.

settingsFileLocations.title: File Locations

settingsFileLocations.mediaServerPathPrefixHelper: Base path where your media server sees your media files (used for symlinks)
settingsFileLocations.detectSuccess: Paths detected! Click "Save Changes" to apply.

settingsLibraryConfig.title: Library Configuration
settingsLibraryConfig.subtitle: Select which libraries to include when syncing content
settingsLibraryConfig.syncTooltip: Refresh library list from media server
settingsLibraryConfig.noLibrariesMovie: No movie libraries found. Click "Sync from Media Server" to fetch your libraries.
settingsLibraryConfig.noLibrariesTv: No TV show libraries found. Click "Sync from Media Server" to fetch your libraries.
settingsLibraryConfig.warningNoneEnabled: ⚠️ No libraries enabled — {{type}} sync will be skipped
settingsLibraryConfig.footerNextSync: Changes take effect on the next {{type}} sync. Run the appropriate sync job after making changes.
settingsLibraryConfig.emptyState: No libraries configured yet. Click "Sync from Media Server" to fetch your libraries.

settingsOutputFormat.title: AI Recommendations Output Format
settingsOutputFormat.subtitle: Choose how AI recommendation files are created for the media server
settingsOutputFormat.loadFailed: Failed to load output format configuration
settingsOutputFormat.moviesSymlinkHelp: Creates symbolic links to original movie files
settingsOutputFormat.moviesStrmHelp: Creates .strm files with paths or streaming URLs
settingsOutputFormat.seriesSymlinkHelp: Creates symlinks to original season folders (recommended)
settingsOutputFormat.seriesStrmRecommendation: Symlinks are recommended for TV series for better performance
settingsOutputFormat.explainSymlinksIntro: Creates symbolic links pointing to your original media files. Faster playback, same quality.
settingsOutputFormat.explainSymlinksRequires: Both Aperture and your media server must access the same filesystem paths.
settingsOutputFormat.explainStrmIntro: Creates .strm files containing paths or streaming URLs. Works in all network configurations.
settingsOutputFormat.explainStrmBestFor: Docker setups, different machines, or when paths differ between systems.
settingsOutputFormat.symlinkWarningBody: Symlinks require that Aperture can access your media files at the exact same paths that your media server uses. If you're running Aperture in Docker, ensure the volume mounts match.

settingsMdblistSelector.manualInfoSuffix: and paste the URL or ID here.
settingsMdblistSelector.emptyMyLists: You don't have any lists on MDBList
settingsMdblistSelector.emptySearchShort: Type at least 2 characters to search
settingsMdblistSelector.errInvalidId: Please enter a valid list ID or MDBList URL

settingsAiSetup.pageSubtitle: Configure AI providers for different features. Aperture recommends using OpenAI for all four functions, but feel free to explore other providers and models.
settingsAiSetup.cardEmbeddingsDesc: Converts text to vectors for semantic search and recommendations. Higher dimensions = better quality.
settingsAiSetup.cardChatDesc: Powers the AI assistant. Needs tool calling to search your library and make recommendations.
settingsAiSetup.cardTextGenDesc: Generates recommendation explanations, taste profiles, and playlist descriptions.
settingsAiSetup.cardExplorationDesc: Powers the Explore page. Uses AI to find meaningful connections from conceptual searches like "feel-good comedies".
settingsAiSetup.embeddingSetsBody: You have embeddings stored from multiple models. Inactive sets can be deleted to free up storage. When you switch back to a model with existing embeddings, they will be used immediately without regenerating.
settingsAiSetup.confirmDeleteSet: Delete all embeddings for "{{model}}"? This cannot be undone.

settingsAiExplanation.loadFailed: Failed to load AI explanation configuration
settingsAiExplanation.title: AI Explanation in Recommendations
settingsAiExplanation.subtitle: Control whether AI-generated explanations ("Why Aperture picked this for you") appear in recommendation descriptions
settingsAiExplanation.includeDesc: When enabled, each recommendation includes a personalized explanation of why it was selected
settingsAiExplanation.overrideDesc: When enabled, administrators can grant individual users the ability to toggle AI explanations for their own recommendations
settingsAiExplanation.infoThreeTierBody: Global default → Admin grants override per user → User chooses their preference.
settingsAiExplanation.infoGrantPath: To grant a user override permission, go to Users → select a user → enable "Allow AI explanation override".

settingsCostEstimator.configureFirst: Configure your AI providers above to see cost estimates.
settingsCostEstimator.title: Cost Estimator
settingsCostEstimator.tooltip: Estimates based on your configured providers. Actual costs may vary.
settingsCostEstimator.localNoteBody: Running AI locally means $0.00 API costs for those functions. Only your compute costs (electricity, hardware) apply.
settingsCostEstimator.initialCaption: One-time cost to embed your entire library ({{movies}} movies, {{series}} series, {{episodes}} episodes)
settingsCostEstimator.recurringCaption: Ongoing costs for recommendations, explanations, and chat

settingsRecAlgo.title: Recommendation Algorithm
settingsRecAlgo.subtitle: Configure how recommendations are generated. Hover the help icons next to each setting for detailed explanations.
settingsRecAlgo.loadFailed: Could not load recommendation configuration. Try refreshing the page.
settingsRecAlgo.footer: Changes apply to the next recommendation generation run.

maxCandidates.title: Max Candidates
maxCandidates.description: How many items from your library to evaluate as potential recommendations.
maxCandidates.increase: More thorough search, considers entire library, but slower processing.
maxCandidates.decrease: Faster generation, but may miss some good matches from less popular items.
maxCandidates.example: Set to ∞ (unlimited) for best results if you have time, or 10-20K for faster daily runs.

selectedCount.title: Recommendations Per User
selectedCount.description: The final number of recommendations each user receives.
selectedCount.increase: More choices for users, but quality of later picks may decrease.
selectedCount.decrease: Fewer but higher-quality, more confident recommendations.
selectedCount.example: Movies: 20-50 is typical. Series: 10-15 since they require more time investment.

recentWatchLimit.title: Watch History Depth
recentWatchLimit.description: How many recently watched items to analyze when building the taste profile.
recentWatchLimit.increase: Broader understanding of long-term preferences, better for established users.
recentWatchLimit.decrease: Focuses on recent viewing habits, good if tastes change frequently.
recentWatchLimit.example: New user with 20 watches? Set to 20. Long-time user? 50-100 captures their full taste.

similarityWeight.title: Taste Similarity
similarityWeight.description: How much to favor items that match the user's established preferences.
similarityWeight.increase: Safer picks the user will likely enjoy, but less adventurous.
similarityWeight.decrease: More variety and surprises, but higher chance of misses.
similarityWeight.example: Set high (50-60%) for picky viewers. Set lower (30-40%) for those who like discovering new things.

noveltyWeight.title: Genre Discovery
noveltyWeight.description: Rewards items with genres the user hasn't explored much.
noveltyWeight.increase: Pushes users outside their comfort zone into new genres.
noveltyWeight.decrease: Sticks to familiar territory and known favorite genres.
noveltyWeight.example: Someone who only watches action? Higher novelty might suggest a thriller or sci-fi they'd enjoy.

ratingWeight.title: Community Rating
ratingWeight.description: How much to favor highly-rated, critically acclaimed items.
ratingWeight.increase: Prioritizes quality over personalization — great for new users.
ratingWeight.decrease: Ignores ratings, trusts the taste-matching algorithm more.
ratingWeight.example: Set higher (30%+) if you want to surface hidden gems with great reviews.

diversityWeight.title: Result Diversity
diversityWeight.description: Ensures variety in the final recommendation list.
diversityWeight.increase: Spreads recommendations across different genres, years, styles.
diversityWeight.decrease: May cluster similar items together if they all score well.
diversityWeight.example: High diversity prevents getting 10 Marvel movies in a row even if the user loves them.

settingsWatching.title: Shows You Watch
settingsWatching.subtitle: Let users track series they are watching, see upcoming episodes, and keep their list in sync with media server favorites (Emby/Jellyfin).
settingsWatching.loadFailed: Failed to load Shows You Watch configuration
settingsWatching.enableCaption: When disabled, the list and favorites sync are unavailable for all users.

settingsLegacyEmbeddings.dialogBody: This will permanently delete the following tables:
settingsLegacyEmbeddings.dialogFooter: This action cannot be undone. Your new dimension-specific embedding tables will not be affected.

settingsPage.personalPrefsFooter: Changes will apply the next time the "Update Permissions" job runs or when recommendations are regenerated. If you already have a library with the old name, you may need to manually delete it from your media server.

assistant.title: Aperture Encore
assistant.subtitle: AI-powered recommendations
assistant.placeholder: Ask me anything about movies or shows…
assistant.welcomeBody: Your AI movie and TV recommendation assistant. Ask me to find something to watch, discover similar titles, or explore your personalized picks!
assistant.suggestion4: What sci-fi movies do you recommend?

welcomeModal.title: Welcome to Aperture
welcomeModal.subtitle: AI-Powered Movie Recommendations
welcomeModal.stepWelcomeP1: <0>Aperture</0> is your personal AI-powered movie recommendation engine that learns from your watch history to suggest films you'll love.
welcomeModal.stepWelcomeP2: It integrates with your Emby or Jellyfin media server to understand your tastes and deliver personalized recommendations directly to your home screen.
welcomeModal.stepHowAiP1: Aperture uses <0>AI embeddings</0> to understand movies at a deep level:
welcomeModal.stepHowAiLi1: <0>Semantic Understanding:</0> Each movie is converted into a 1,536-dimensional vector that captures its themes, tone, genre, and style.
welcomeModal.stepHowAiLi2: <0>Taste Profile:</0> Your watch history is analyzed to build a unique "taste fingerprint" based on what you've enjoyed.
welcomeModal.stepHowAiLi3: <0>Vector Similarity:</0> We find movies whose embeddings are mathematically closest to your taste profile.
welcomeModal.stepHowAiP2: This means recommendations go beyond simple genre matching — a psychological thriller fan might get suggestions for tense dramas with similar themes, even if they're not labeled as "thrillers."
welcomeModal.stepScoringP1: Each recommended movie is scored using multiple factors:
welcomeModal.stepScoringTasteD: How closely the movie matches your overall taste profile
welcomeModal.stepScoringGenreD: Rewards movies that introduce you to new genres while staying relevant
welcomeModal.stepScoringCommunityD: Favors highly-rated films to ensure quality recommendations
welcomeModal.stepScoringDiversityD: Ensures variety so you don't get 50 superhero movies in a row
welcomeModal.stepSimilarP1: When viewing a movie's detail page, you'll see <0>"Similar Movies"</0> powered by the same AI technology.
welcomeModal.stepSimilarP2: These aren't just movies in the same genre — they're films with similar:
welcomeModal.stepSimilarP3: So if you loved "Inception," you might see mind-bending films that share its cerebral nature, not just other Christopher Nolan movies.
welcomeModal.stepDataP1: Aperture syncs with your media server to understand your preferences:
welcomeModal.stepDataLi1: <0>Watch History:</0> Movies you've watched, including play counts and recency
welcomeModal.stepDataLi2: <0>Favorites:</0> Movies you've marked as favorites get extra weight
welcomeModal.stepDataLi3: <0>Recency:</0> Recent watches influence your taste profile more than older ones
welcomeModal.stepDataP2: You can choose to include or exclude already-watched movies from your recommendations in your preferences.

explorationConfig.title: New Feature: Exploration AI
explorationConfig.subtitle: Configure a dedicated AI for the Explore page
explorationConfig.bodyP1: Aperture now supports a dedicated <0>Exploration AI provider</0> for the Explore page's semantic graph generation.
explorationConfig.feature1Body: Use a more powerful model to understand queries like "feel-good comedies" or "mind-bending sci-fi"
explorationConfig.feature2Body: AI analyzes your library to find meaningful thematic connections between items
explorationConfig.feature3Body: Use GPT-4 or Claude for exploration while keeping cheaper models for other functions

myRecommendations.subtitle: AI-powered picks personalized for your taste

myRecommendations.emptySeries: No series recommendations generated yet. Your personalized picks will appear here once an admin runs the recommendation job.

myRequests.pageSubtitle: Content you requested through Discovery or Gap Analysis (admin). Status is synced from Seerr when available.

watchHistoryPage.title: Watch History
watchHistoryPage.emptyMovies: No watch history found. Movies you watch will appear here after the watch history sync runs.
watchHistoryPage.emptySeries: No watch history found. Series you watch will appear here after the watch history sync runs.
watchHistoryPage.confirmMovieBody: This will mark "{{title}}" as unwatched in your media server and remove it from your Aperture watch history.
watchHistoryPage.confirmSeriesBody: This will mark all episodes of "{{title}}" as unwatched in your media server and remove them from your Aperture watch history.

topPicksPage.subtitleMovies: Most popular movies on your server
topPicksPage.subtitleSeries: Most popular TV series on your server
topPicksPage.rankedSubtitle: Ranked by popularity based on watch activity from all users over the last {{days}} days
topPicksPage.rankedSubtitleNoWindow: Ranked by popularity based on watch activity from all users
topPicksPage.emptyMovies: No trending movies yet. Top picks are calculated from watch history across all users. Make sure watch history sync has run and multiple users have watched some content.
topPicksPage.emptySeries: No trending series yet. Top picks are calculated from watch history across all users. Make sure TV series watch history sync has run and multiple users have watched some episodes.
